beats.text: A GAN sets up two networks as opponents and trains them together until fake samples become indistinguishable from real data.
Pixel-wise reconstruction losses reward blur, because averaging two plausible faces gives low error and a smeared ghost.
The generator maps a latent vector drawn from a fixed Gaussian prior into a sample in data space.
The discriminator takes any sample and outputs its estimated probability that the input is real rather than generated.
The generator never sees a real image directly; its only signal is the gradient flowing back through a moving discriminator.
A vanilla GAN is an implicit model: you can sample from it freely, but it cannot score the likelihood of a held-out image.
Both players are packaged into a single two-player value function optimized in opposite directions.
The discriminator maximizes and the generator minimizes the value function over real data and over noise.
For a fixed generator the optimal discriminator is the posterior probability that a sample is real under the two densities.
When the generator matches the data, the most informed verdict on every input is exactly one half, a coin flip.
Substituting the optimal discriminator, the generator is minimizing the Jensen-Shannon divergence, with global minimum minus log four.
In practice the generator maximizes log D of its samples, the non-saturating form that gives strong gradients while it is losing.
The theory assumes the inner maximization is solved exactly and the distributions overlap; reality grants neither.
When supports barely overlap, the Jensen-Shannon divergence is pinned at its ceiling of log two, so the gradient vanishes.
Mode collapse: the generator can win by mapping every latent to a single hyper-realistic output and abandoning the rest.
Collapse is structural: the adversarial loss has no term demanding diversity, so covering one mode scores as well as all.
Simultaneous gradient descent on a minimax game can orbit the equilibrium forever, like players in rock-paper-scissors.
A generator loss that falls smoothly to zero usually means the discriminator collapsed, not that training succeeded.
Two papers turned the fragile original GAN into something reliable: one fixed the architecture, one fixed the loss.
DCGAN made image GANs reproducible with strided convolutions, transposed convolutions, batch norm, and no fully connected layers.
WGAN measures distance with the Wasserstein earth-mover's metric, which stays smooth even when supports do not overlap.
The intractable infimum becomes a maximization over one-Lipschitz functions, repurposing the discriminator as an unbounded critic.
Because the critic estimates a genuine distance, its value correlates with sample quality, giving a loss curve you can read.
Across variants, WGAN and WGAN-GP swap Jensen-Shannon for Wasserstein and a probability output for a real-valued critic.
By 2018 GANs generated small images reliably; the open question was control and resolution.
Progressive growing added resolution layers one at a time, reaching one thousand twenty four by one thousand twenty four images.
StyleGAN maps the latent into an intermediate code that modulates feature-map statistics at every resolution via adaptive instance norm.
Coarse layers set pose and shape, middle layers set features, and fine layers set color and micro-texture.
After 2021 diffusion models largely displaced GANs for image synthesis, being easier to train and covering modes better.
GANs survive where latency matters, generating in a single forward pass and distilling slow diffusion into one-step models.
